raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Geothermal power is key to achieving 100% clean energy around the clock.",
    "Geothermal energy is critical for California’s long-term energy stability and environmental responsibility.",
    "Geothermal energy provides clean, continuous power, unlike wind and solar energy.",
    "California has a unique opportunity to expand its geothermal infrastructure to support its transition to carbon-free energy.",
    "Geothermal energy currently accounts for only 5% of California’s in-state electricity generation.",
    "Next-generation geothermal projects can take a decade or more to develop.",
    "The U.S. Department of the Interior is seeking to expand a categorical exclusion from NEPA for geothermal resource confirmation activities on federal lands.",
    "Geothermal exploratory projects can meet the same environmental standards as determined by the lead agency overseeing the project to be compliant with CEQA.",
    "The CEC created a process for expedited judicial review under CEQA for certain projects in 2011.",
    "AB 531 would make geothermal power plants and geothermal field development projects under 50 megawatts eligible for streamlined processes."
  ]
}
```
